{{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}} [[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]] {{Buddhism}}
{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[Pure Land]] [[Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitabha]] [[Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[Tendai]] [[Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]
[[Category:Pure Land Buddhism]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]‎]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]]] [[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] {{Mahayana-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}} [[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]] {{Buddhism}}
{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[Pure Land]] [[Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitabha]] [[Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[Tendai]] [[Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]

{{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}} [[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]] {{Buddhism}}
{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[Pure Land]] [[Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitabha]] [[Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[Tendai]] [[Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]

== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[Pure Land]]. {{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[Pure Land]] [[Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitabha]] [[Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[Tendai]] [[Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]

{{Buddhism}}

{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[:en:Nianfo|Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitābha]] [[:en:Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.

== History ==
The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[:en:Ryōnin|Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].

== Practice ==
The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]].  A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.

{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[:en:Nianfo|Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitābha]] [[:en:Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[:en:Ryōnin|Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]

{{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}}
[[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]]
{{Buddhism}}

{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[:en:Nianfo|Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitābha]] [[:en:Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.

== History ==
The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[:en:Ryōnin|Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].

== Practice ==
The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]].  A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner. 

==Notes==

==References==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q578719]] Wikidata name: Yuzu Nembutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}} [[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]] {{Buddhism}}
{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[:en:Nianfo|Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitābha]] [[:en:Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[:en:Ryōnin|Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
‎]] {{Mahayana-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}

Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.

A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Yūzū Nembutsu-shū]][[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]][[fr:Yūzū nembutsu shū]][[ja:融通念仏宗]][[no:Yūzū Nembutsu]][[pl:Yūzū nembutsu]][[ru:Юдзу-нэмбуцу]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q578719]] Wikidata name: Yuzu Nembutsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Jodo School focused on interconnectedness of Nenbutsu}} [[File:Dainenbutsuji01s2048.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]], the head temple of Yuzu Nembutsu.]] {{Buddhism}}
{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or [[:en:Nianfo|Nianfo]]), the name of the [[Amitābha]] [[:en:Buddhahood|Buddha]]. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named [[:en:Ryōnin|Ryōnin]] (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at [[:en:Dainenbutsu-ji|Dainenbutsu-ji]] in [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]].
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]]. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
‎]] {{Mahayana-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}
[[en:Yuzu Nembutsu]]

{{nihongo|'''Yuzu Nembutsu'''|融通念仏宗|Yūzū-nenbutsu-shū}} is a school of {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} that focuses on the ritual recitation of the ''Nembutsu'' (or {{ill|Nianfo|en|Nianfo|WD=Q1315754|lt=Nianfo}}), the name of the [[Amitābha]] {{ill|Buddhahood|en|Buddhahood|WD=Q56247062|lt=Buddha}}. Followers believe this recitation benefits not just the speaker, but the entire world as well.
== History == The sect began in the twelfth century when a {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} [[:en:Buddhist monk|monk]] named {{ill|Ryōnin|en|Ryōnin|WD=Q11614413|lt=Ryōnin}} (良忍, 1072–1132) wrote a commentary on rituals and hymns in practice at the time and founded the school in 1117, with its headquarters located at {{ill|Dainenbutsu-ji|en|Dainenbutsu-ji|WD=Q1157628|lt=Dainenbutsu-ji}} in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka}}.
== Practice == The distinction of this Pure Land sect lies in its emphasis on the nature of interconnectedness amongst phenomena.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20140519074832/http://dictionary.buddhistdoor.com/en/word/16728/yuzu-nembutsu%20school Buddhist Dictionary], Tung Lin Kok Yuen Buddhist Door</ref> The recitation of the nembutsu was not merely an individual quest for salvation in the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}}. A practitioner's chants of nembutsu benefitted everybody else and everybody else's chants of nembutsu benefitted the solo practitioner.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
==References== {{Commons Category|Yūzū Nembutsu Buddhism}} *[http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/655142/Yuzu-Nembutsu Encyclopædia Britannica entry]
‎]] {{Mahayana-stub}} {{Japan-reli-stub}}
